title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding
task: 3D scene understanding and spatial reasoning for MLLMs
key_results: Outperforms state-of-the-art on 3D scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation benchmarks without explicit 3D supervision
comments: Clever repurposing of video diffusion models' implicit spatial priors; demonstrates that generative models learn robust physical priors as a byproduct of temporal coherence.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video generation models learn implicit 3D structural priors from temporal coherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; extracted spatiotemporal features improve MLLM spatial reasoning

title: SAMA: Factorized Semantic Anchoring and Motion Alignment for Instruction-Guided Video Editing
task: Instruction-guided video editing with semantic-motion factorization
key_results: Achieves state-of-the-art among open-source and competitive with commercial systems; factorized pre-training alone yields strong zero-shot editing
comments: The factorization paradigm elegantly separates semantic understanding from temporal dynamics, avoiding explicit external priors that limit generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoupling semantic anchoring and motion modeling improves editing quality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot ability validates the factorization approach

title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
task: Real-time vision-language-action inference with low latency
key_results: Compresses immediate reaction denoising tenfold into single step; enables real-time robotics on consumer GPUs with table-tennis task validation
comments: Addresses a practical bottleneck in VLA deployment; Horizon-Aware Schedule is elegant but relies on action chunking assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constant sampling schedules are inefficient for reaction latency in flow-based VLAs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; adaptive prioritization of near-term actions reduces reaction latency

title: 3DreamBooth: High-Fidelity 3D Subject-Driven Video Generation Model
task: 3D-aware subject-driven video generation
key_results: Achieves view-consistent video generation of customized 3D subjects via 1-frame optimization; enables multi-view adaptation without exhaustive video training
comments: Novel insight that single-frame optimization with geometric conditioning can avoid temporal overfitting; asymmetrical multi-view strategy is well-motivated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Restricting spatial updates in 1-frame optimization prevents temporal overfitting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; robust 3D priors learned without multi-view video data

title: Bridging Semantic and Kinematic Conditions with Diffusion-based Discrete Motion Tokenizer
task: Human motion generation with semantic and kinematic control
key_results: Reduces trajectory error from 0.72 to 0.08 cm and FID from 0.083 to 0.029 on HumanML3D using one-sixth of tokens; improves under stronger constraints
comments: Bridging discrete tokens and continuous diffusion is elegant; the three-stage framework cleanly separates concerns.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Delegating motion recovery to diffusion decoder enables compact tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; single-layer tokens preserve fidelity with 83% token reduction

title: MonoArt: Progressive Structural Reasoning for Monocular Articulated 3D Reconstruction
task: Monocular articulated 3D object reconstruction
key_results: State-of-the-art on PartNet-Mobility in reconstruction accuracy and inference speed; generalizes to robotic manipulation and articulated scenes
comments: Progressive structural reasoning avoids direct articulation regression; missing quantitative results and comparison metrics in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Progressive transformation into canonical geometry enables stable articulation inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms multi-view and retrieval-based baselines

title: Cubic Discrete Diffusion: Discrete Visual Generation on High-Dimensional Representation Tokens
task: Discrete visual generation on high-dimensional representation tokens
key_results: First discrete generation model for high-dimensional (768-1024 dim) tokens; state-of-the-art on ImageNet-256 with strong scaling (900M to 3.7B); validates discretized tokens preserve original capabilities
comments: Enabling unified multimodal architectures via discrete high-dim tokens is ambitious; fine-grained masking approach is well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-dimensional discrete tokens can preserve representation capabilities for both understanding and generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; same tokens effective for dual tasks

title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs
task: Evaluating omnimodal LLMs on long-form audio-video understanding
key_results: Gemini 3 Pro achieves ~65% accuracy; open-source models <35%; reveals challenges in long-term memory, temporal localization, and multimodal perception
comments: Critical gap identified between short-clip and real-world long-form video; benchmark is valuable but shows existing models are far from production-ready.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current OmniLLMs struggle with long-form audio-visual understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant accuracy gaps across all tested models

title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents
task: Continual agent self-improvement via skill design
key_results: 26.2% and 116.2% relative accuracy improvements on General AI Assistants and Humanity's Last Exam; agent autonomously designs agents for new tasks
comments: Memory-based RL with externalised skills enables parameter-free adaptation; impressive but lacks analysis of failure modes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents can autonomously design task-specific agents through iterative skill refinement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; sustained gains across benchmarks

title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World
task: Multilingual embedding models for 200+ languages
key_results: F2LLM-v2-14B ranks first on 11 MTEB benchmarks; smaller models achieve SOTA for resource-constrained settings; supports 200+ languages including low-resource
comments: Matryoshka learning + pruning + distillation is effective recipe; emphasis on underserved languages is important.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two-stage LLM-based training with matryoshka learning enables efficient multilingual embeddings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA across diverse model sizes

title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation
task: Post-training compact MoE LLMs with cascade RL
key_results: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (30B MoE, 3B active) achieves Gold in IMO, IOI, ICPC with 20x fewer parameters than DeepSeekV3.2; competitive with frontier models
comments: Multi-domain on-policy distillation during RL is clever for recovering regressions; exceptional density-to-capability ratio.

model_io.model: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (MoE with cascade RL + multi-domain distillation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cascade RL + multi-domain on-policy distillation sustains gains in compact MoE models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong reasoning with high parameter efficiency

title: ReactMotion: Generating Reactive Listener Motions from Speaker Utterance
task: Generate reactive listener body motions from speaker utterances
key_results: ReactMotion outperforms retrieval baselines and LLM pipelines on naturalness, diversity, and appropriateness metrics; dataset ReactMotionNet contains speaker-listener motion pairs with multi-level appropriateness annotations
comments: The preference-based training approach is well-motivated for modeling the inherent non-determinism of human reactions, though generalization beyond the dataset domain remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preference-based training enables more appropriate and diverse listener motion generation than single-ground-truth supervision
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ReactMotion outperforms baselines in appropriateness and diversity

title: Cognitive Mismatch in Multimodal Large Language Models for Discrete Symbol Understanding
task: Evaluating MLLMs on discrete symbol understanding
key_results: Models fail at basic symbol recognition but succeed at complex reasoning; open-source models <35%, frontier models ~65-75%; reveals cognitive mismatch
comments: Uncovers fundamental limitation: MLLMs rely on linguistic probability rather than true visual perception of symbols; important diagnostic.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLMs exhibit cognitive mismatch in discrete symbol understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; fail basic recognition but succeed at complex reasoning

title: AndroTMem: From Interaction Trajectories to Anchored Memory in Long-Horizon GUI Agents
task: Long-horizon GUI interaction with effective memory mechanisms for Android tasks
key_results: Anchored State Memory (ASM) improves Task Complete Rate by 5%-30.16% over full-sequence and summary baselines on 1,069 tasks; identifies within-task memory failures as primary performance bottleneck
comments: The diagnostic framework effectively isolates memory as the limiting factor in long-horizon GUI agents, though ASM's reliance on identifying critical intermediate states may require task-specific tuning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Performance degradation in long-horizon tasks is primarily due to memory failures rather than perception or action errors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across 12 GUI agents; ASM addresses this bottleneck

title: EffectErase: Joint Video Object Removal and Insertion for High-Quality Effect Erasing
task: Video object removal with effect erasing and consistent background synthesis
key_results: VOR dataset: 60K paired videos, 5 effect types; EffectErase achieves superior performance in effect-aware removal via reciprocal insertion-removal learning
comments: Dataset addresses real gap in video inpainting; treating insertion as auxiliary task is well-motivated for learning complementary behaviors.

outputs: video with object removed and background restored

hypotheses.hypothesis: Insertion as auxiliary task improves removal quality through complementary learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; insertion-removal consistency objective enhances effect erasing

title: SimulU: Training-free Policy for Long-form Simultaneous Speech-to-Speech Translation
task: Training-free policy for long-form simultaneous speech-to-speech translation
key_results: SimulU achieves comparable quality-latency trade-off to cascaded baselines without training on MuST-C across 8 languages; first training-free approach for long-form SimulS2S
comments: Leveraging cross-attention in pre-trained models for policy derivation is elegant but the approach's robustness to model architecture variations and truly long-form scenarios (>30 minutes) requires further validation.

outputs: translated speech stream with controlled latency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-attention patterns in pre-trained end-to-end models can be exploited to formulate effective simultaneous translation policies without additional training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SimulU matches cascaded baselines on quality-latency tradeoffs

title: VTC-Bench: Evaluating Agentic Multimodal Models via Compositional Visual Tool Chaining
task: Evaluating agentic MLLMs on tool composition and visual reasoning
key_results: Gemini-3.0-Pro achieves 51% on VTC-Bench; reveals struggles in tool generalization and multi-tool composition; 680 problems across 9-category hierarchy
comments: Comprehensive benchmark with realistic OpenCV tools; exposes that models rely on familiar subsets rather than selecting optimal tools.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with generalization to diverse tool-sets and multi-tool composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; limited to familiar tools, inefficient planning

title: MOSS-TTS Technical Report
task: Speech generation with token-based autoregressive modeling
key_results: Supports zero-shot voice cloning, token-level duration control, pronunciation control, code-switching, long-form generation; two model variants for scalability vs. efficiency
comments: Discrete audio tokens + causal tokenizer is sound recipe; variable-bitrate RVQ for semantic-acoustic coupling is well-designed.

model_io.model: MOSS-TTS (autoregressive speech generator on discrete tokens)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal tokenizer with variable-bitrate RVQ enables unified semantic-acoustic representation for zero-shot voice cloning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; supports multilingual and long-form generation

title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
task: Scalable rollout infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents
key_results: ProRL Agent enables RL training on software engineering, math, STEM, and coding; supports rootless HPC environments; rollout-as-a-service design decouples orchestration
comments: Infrastructure contribution; clean separation of concerns (rollout vs. training) improves maintainability but limited novelty in RL itself.

model_io.model: ProRL Agent (RL infrastructure for agentic rollouts)

title: Tinted Frames: Question Framing Blinds Vision-Language Models
task: Improve visual grounding in Vision-Language Models through prompt tuning
key_results: Constrained question framing (yes/no, multiple choice) reduces visual attention by substantial margins; learnable token prompts restore visually grounded attention patterns and improve cross-framing consistency
comments: The mechanistic finding that VLMs modulate attention based on linguistic framing is insightful, but the lightweight tuning method's effectiveness on more complex vision-reasoning tasks beyond the tested scope remains open.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Question framing causes selective attention blindness in VLMs, with constrained framings inducing lower image attention
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via attention quantification; learnable tokens mitigate this effect

title: OSM-based Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing VLMs
task: Domain adaptation of VLMs for remote sensing without teacher models
key_results: OSMDA-VLM achieves SOTA on 10 benchmarks; eliminates dependency on frontier teacher models; generates annotations via OSM pairing without manual labels
comments: Self-contained framework using OSM as annotation engine is clever and scalable; demonstrates that auxiliary geographic data replaces need for stronger teachers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: OSM-based pseudo-labeling enables domain adaptation without external teacher models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with lower training cost

title: Loc3R-VLM: Language-based Localization and 3D Reasoning with Vision-Language Models
task: Language-based localization and 3D reasoning with monocular video
key_results: SOTA on language-based localization and 3D QA benchmarks; outperforms 2D and video baselines via global layout + egocentric perspective supervision
comments: Joint objectives for layout reconstruction and situation modeling elegantly ground language in 3D; lightweight camera pose priors from foundation models is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global layout + egocentric supervision enables VLMs to reason about 3D space
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA on localization and 3D QA tasks

title: MHPO: Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization for Stable Reinforcement Learning
task: Stable reinforcement learning with hazard-aware policy optimization
key_results: MHPO outperforms existing methods on reasoning benchmarks (text and vision-language); Log-Fidelity Modulator enables differentiable ratio control; asymmetric hazard penalties prevent mode collapse
comments: Survival analysis for hazard modeling is creative; addresses real instability issues in GRPO but lacks comparison with recent PPO variants.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Log-Fidelity Modulator + Decoupled Hazard Penalty improve training stability and prevent mode collapse
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent gains on diverse benchmarks

title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation
task: Mathematical reasoning with formal object derivation and test-time aggregation
key_results: Strong LMs (Qwen3-235B, o3) struggle on Principia; on-policy judge training boosts performance; test-time aggregation scales compute; cross-format generalization to numerical/MCQA
comments: Principia benchmark addresses real gap in formal reasoning; on-policy judge training is well-motivated but evaluation limited to few models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On-policy judge training improves mathematical object reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; boosts performance on Principia and numerical tasks

title: Matryoshka Gaussian Splatting
task: Level-of-detail rendering for 3D Gaussian Splatting
key_results: Continuous LoD without sacrificing full-capacity quality; stochastic budget training maintains backbone performance; smooth speed-quality trade-off from single model
comments: Clever stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without architecture changes; useful for practical 3D applications.

model_io.model: MGS (ordered Gaussian splatting with stochastic training)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without quality degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; matches full-capacity performance across budgets

title: Prompt-Free Universal Region Proposal Network
task: Prompt-free universal object proposal generation
key_results: Works with 5% of COCO data; generalizes to 19 datasets without fine-tuning (underwater, industrial defect, remote sensing); no external prompts required
comments: Learnable query embeddings with visual feature coupling and cascade self-prompting is effective; impressive zero-shot generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-prompted learnable embeddings enable prompt-free object detection generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot transfer across domains

title: Mending the Holes: Mitigating Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Translation
task: Low-resource multilingual machine translation via RL with quality estimation
key_results: WALAR improves 1400 language directions on Flores-101; outperforms LLaMAX; retains high-resource performance while elevating low-resource translation
comments: Insight about QE model failure modes ('holes') motivating word/language alignment is practical; RL-only approach using monolingual data is resource-efficient.

model_io.model: LLM with RL (QE-based reward, word/language alignment)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitigating QE model holes via word/language alignment improves low-resource translation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; large gains on 1400 Flores-101 directions

title: PARSA-Bench: A Comprehensive Persian Audio-Language Model Benchmark
task: Evaluate audio-language models on Persian language and cultural understanding
key_results: PARSA-Bench comprises 16 tasks with 8,000+ samples; text-only baselines outperform audio models; all models achieve near-random performance on prosodic vazn detection
comments: The benchmark effectively exposes a critical gap in prosodic perception across model scales, highlighting a fundamental limitation of current audio-language models rather than a training or dataset issue.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current audio-language models do not effectively leverage audio-specific information beyond what transcription provides
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; text-only baselines consistently outperform audio counterparts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prosodic understanding (vazn detection) is beyond reach of current models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; all models perform near random on culturally-grounded prosodic tasks

title: VID-AD: A Dataset for Image-Level Logical Anomaly Detection under Vision-Induced Distraction
task: Image-level logical anomaly detection under visual distraction in manufacturing
key_results: VID-AD dataset contains 10,395 images across 50 one-class tasks with controlled visual distraction; language-based framework with contrastive learning outperforms baselines by capturing logical attributes over low-level features
comments: Using text descriptions of normal images to synthesize negative examples via contradiction is a clever data augmentation approach, but the framework's transferability to novel manufacturing scenarios beyond the 10 included remains untested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contrastive learning on logical attributes (vs. low-level visual features) improves robustness to vision-induced distraction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; text-based embeddings improve performance across distraction conditions

title: COT-FM: Cluster-wise Optimal Transport Flow Matching
task: Faster flow matching via cluster-wise optimal transport
key_results: COT-FM accelerates sampling and improves generation quality; straightens vector fields via cluster-wise coupling; plug-and-play across 2D, image, and robotics tasks
comments: Cluster-wise OT to improve flow straightness is intuitive; no architectural changes required, making adoption practical.

model_io.model: COT-FM (flow matching with optimal transport reshaping)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster-wise OT yields straighter vector fields and faster convergence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent acceleration and quality improvement

title: Structural basis for prostaglandin and drug transport via SLCO2A1
task: Elucidate substrate selectivity and drug binding mechanisms in SLCO2A1 prostaglandin transporter
key_results: Cryo-EM structures reveal PG and NSAID binding sites; MD simulations and cellular assays define molecular basis for PG/drug recognition and SLCO subfamily adaptation
comments: Multi-method approach (structural, computational, functional) addresses broad drug-metabolite selectivity question relevant to personalized medicine.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SLCO2A1 substrate selectivity reflects specific molecular recognition of PGs and NSAIDs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; structures and simulations define selectivity basis

title: Structural mechanism of substrate binding and inhibition of human taurine transporter
task: Elucidate substrate binding and inhibition mechanisms of human taurine transporter
key_results: Structures of hTauT in four states reveal taurine, β-alanine, and P4S inhibitor bind at central substrate-binding site; TM1a bending opens intracellular gate for ion/substrate release
comments: Multiple ligand-bound structures provide comprehensive mechanistic detail; clinical relevance to neurological and retinal diseases strengthens impact.

hypotheses.hypothesis: β-alanine and P4S occupy the same binding site as taurine
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; both inhibitor and alternative substrate mimic taurine binding

title: A single cluster of RNA Polymerase II molecules is stably associated with active genes
task: Characterize RNAPII cluster formation, stability, and functional association with active genes
key_results: Single RNAPII clusters stably associate with active gene loci; cluster formation depends on transcription initiation; cluster lifetimes correlate with transcriptional activity; intensities strongly predict transcriptional output
comments: Single-molecule tracking during ZGA provides developmental context; data argue against phase separation model and support local polymerase accumulation mechanism.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNAPII clusters form via phase separation and are dynamic assemblies
hypotheses.result: Refuted; clusters reflect local accumulations of engaged polymerases without higher-order phase separation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster intensity correlates with transcriptional output
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong correlation observed

title: Dielectric double shell characterization of yeast cells exposed to simulated microgravity
task: Characterize morphological and membrane changes in yeast cells under simulated microgravity
key_results: Dielectrophoresis reveals reduced membrane folding factor and altered permittivity/conductivity under microgravity; impaired nutrient uptake and energy production
comments: Double-shell dielectric model provides quantitative metrics for structural changes, though mechanistic link to metabolic impairment remains indirect.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Microgravity alters yeast cell membrane structure and reduces folding factor
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; microgravity reduces membrane folding and alters electrical properties

title: Soft bionic actuation explains the functional role of whisking in seal whisker sensing
task: Determine functional role of undulatory morphology and active whisking in seal hydrodynamic sensing
key_results: Undulated seal whiskers exhibit 3-fold lower vortex-induced vibrations and >50-fold higher SNR than sea lion whiskers; active whisking essential for prey trail tracking
comments: Integration of natural whiskers with soft actuators and biomimetic muzzle provides strong evidence for functional synergy of morphology and behavior.

model_io.model: Natural seal and sea lion whiskers
Bionic seal muzzle with 30 whiskers per side

hypotheses.hypothesis: Undulatory morphology suppresses self-induced vibrations and active whisking probes surrounding water
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; morphology and active protraction both essential for high SNR

title: A computational analysis of biophysical and geometric constraints refutes existing hypotheses of cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia
task: Refute existing mechanistic hypotheses for cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia
key_results: Computational analysis shows no single proposed mechanism or combination thereof adequately explains cross-excitation in normal DRG conditions
comments: Negative result highlighting gap in understanding sensory processing; suggests unknown biophysical mechanism requiring further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Existing mechanisms (individual or combined) explain cross-excitation
hypotheses.result: Refuted; none adequately describe phenomenon under normal conditions

title: Loss of Pum2 exacerbates colitis by disrupting macrophage–epithelial crosstalk and promoting epithelial necroptosis
task: Identify PUMILIO2 as regulator of intestinal inflammation and determine mechanisms of macrophage-epithelial crosstalk in colitis
key_results: Reduced Pum2 expression in UC patient tissues inversely correlated with disease activity; Pum2 deficiency exacerbated DSS-induced colitis, driving macrophage hyperactivation and TNFα-dependent epithelial necroptosis
comments: The identification of Pum2 as an RNA-binding protein regulating macrophage-epithelial interactions is novel, though the specific Pum2 target RNAs controlling this crosstalk remain to be identified.

model_io.model: DSS-induced colitis model; Pum2-deficient mice; macrophage-epithelial coculture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pum2 downregulation during colitis promotes macrophage hyperactivation and epithelial necroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Pum2 deficiency exacerbated colitis with heightened macrophage inflammation and epithelial necroptosis

title: A cell cycle-dependent transition of acetylation to phosphorylation regulates timely centrosome maturation
task: Elucidate cell cycle control of centrosome maturation timing
key_results: CDK1 phosphorylates RNF40 at T529/T557 in G2/M to recruit PLK1; acetylation-to-phosphorylation transition of RNF40 ensures timely centrosome maturation and bipolar spindle assembly
comments: The acetylation-phosphorylation switch is an elegant mechanism, though in vivo validation in whole organisms would strengthen the work.

model_io.model: HeLa cells synchronized in cell cycle phases

hypotheses.hypothesis: CDK1-mediated RNF40 phosphorylation primes PLK1 recruitment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: phosphorylation at T529/T557 promotes RNF40-PLK1 binding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Acetylation-to-phosphorylation transition regulates timing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: constitutive acetylation or deficient phosphorylation impairs centrosome maturation

title: AP-1 mediates cellular adaptation and memory formation
task: Elucidate AP-1-dependent cellular memory formation during drug resistance development
key_results: Cancer cells form and maintain cellular memories when becoming drug resistant; AP-1-dependent cis epigenetics encodes transient gene expression into persistent chromatin accessibility; low-dose therapy exposure enables adaptation to high-dose challenge
comments: The identification of cis epigenetics in drug resistance is novel, though distinguishing between true epigenetic memory and stochastic selection of pre-existing resistant clones requires single-cell lineage tracing.

model_io.model: Cancer cells exposed to graded doses of therapeutic agents

hypotheses.hypothesis: AP-1 encodes transient therapy-induced gene expression into cis epigenetic memory
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: two-color AP-1 reporter showed cis-encoded memory; chromatin accessibility persisted after stress

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low-dose therapy primes cells to resist high-dose challenge
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: cells adapted during low-dose exposure became resistant to high-dose therapy

title: Mitorubin, berberrubine-based compounds that improve mitochondrial function, exhibit cardioprotective effects against age-related cardiac dysfunction
task: Develop berberrubine-based mitochondrial activators for age-related cardiac dysfunction
key_results: Mitorubin (water-soluble berberrubine derivative) enhanced MITOL expression, increased mitochondrial DNA and protein expression, restored cardiac function, and extended lifespan in HFD-fed aged mice without shortening lifespan in normal aged mice
comments: The lifespan extension in HFD mice is encouraging but mechanistic clarity on MITOL-mediated mitochondrial dynamics would strengthen the translational potential.

model_io.model: Cultured cardiomyocytes; aged mice; HFD-fed mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitorubin promotes MITOL expression to enhance mitochondrial function
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Mitorubin increased MITOL, mitochondrial DNA, protein expression, and respiration

hypotheses.hypothesis: Improved mitochondrial function restores age-related cardiac dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Mitorubin improved cardiac performance and suppressed hypertrophy in aged mice

title: Visualizing suborganellar lipid distribution using correlative light and electron microscopy
task: Develop correlative light and electron microscopy workflow to visualize lipid nanodomain distribution in organellar membranes
key_results: Lipid-CLEM workflow enables quantification of relative lipid densities in membrane nanodomains; differential sphingomyelin partitioning into intraluminal vesicles, recycling tubules, and boundary membrane of early endosome
comments: The Lipid-CLEM methodology is technically innovative and addresses a significant gap in nanoscale lipid visualization, though throughput and accessibility for broader adoption may be limited.

outputs: relative lipid partitioning in membrane nanodomains

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid-CLEM enables nanoscale visualization of lipid organization in membrane compartments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: workflow revealed differential sphingomyelin partitioning in early endosome subdomains

title: Melanin concentrating hormone regulates bone cell activities and calcium metabolism in regenerating goldfish scales
task: Determine MCH-mediated regulation of bone cell activities and calcium metabolism in scale regeneration
key_results: Single MCH treatment suppressed osteoclast activity genes and plasma calcium; continuous MCH treatment elevated both osteoclast and osteoblast activities but decreased scale calcium content; MCH receptor 2 involved in regulation
comments: The biphasic response to MCH (single vs. continuous dosing) suggests temporal dynamics, though the physiological relevance to mammalian bone regeneration and whether goldfish scales fully recapitulate mammalian ossification mechanisms remain unclear.

model_io.model: Cultured goldfish scales; goldfish administered MCH intraperitoneally

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single MCH treatment inhibits osteoclast activity and reduces plasma calcium
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MCH suppressed osteoclast gene expression and decreased TRAP activity and plasma calcium

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prolonged MCH treatment affects both osteoclasts and osteoblasts, reducing scale calcification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: continuous MCH elevated both TRAP and alkaline phosphatase but decreased scale calcium

title: Hypoxia-induced gene expression changes in N. vectensis embryos
task: Investigate O2-dependent embryonic development and hypoxia response in early metazoans
key_results: N. vectensis embryogenesis is O2-dependent; hypoxia induces reversible developmental arrest; transcriptomic profiling reveals conserved hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways shared with bilaterians
comments: The conservation of hypoxia responses across cnidarians and bilaterians is significant for understanding metazoan evolution, though functional validation of specific hypoxia-responsive genes would enhance mechanistic insight.

model_io.model: N. vectensis embryos exposed to normoxia and hypoxia

hypotheses.hypothesis: Early metazoan embryos exhibit conserved hypoxia responses with bilaterians
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways are conserved in N. vectensis embryos

title: Thioredoxin protects against diabetic hearing loss by regulating TOMM22 mediated mitochondrial autophagy in hair cells and inhibiting microglial M1 polarization
task: Elucidate thioredoxin protection against diabetic hearing loss via mitochondrial and microglial mechanisms
key_results: Trx1 overexpression inhibited PINK1/Parkin-mediated mitophagy and reduced apoptosis via TOMM22 maintenance; Trx1 promoted M2 microglial polarization, indirectly protecting auditory cells
comments: The dual mechanism (cell-autonomous and non-cell-autonomous) is comprehensive, though the clinical relevance of serum Trx as a biomarker needs validation in larger cohorts.

model_io.model: Diabetic mice; HEI-OC1 hair cell line; microglial coculture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx regulates TOMM22 to suppress PINK1/Parkin-mediated mitophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: TOMM22 inhibition reversed Trx1 protective effects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx drives microglial M2 polarization to improve auditory microenvironment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Trx1 induced M2 phenotype; coculture with Trx1-treated microglia protected HEI-OC1 cells

title: Collagen-specific molecular chaperone Hsp47 in inguinal white adipose tissue promotes high-fat diet-induced inflammatory gene expression in male mice
task: Determine Hsp47 contribution to HFD-induced adipose tissue inflammation and fibrosis
key_results: Adipocyte-specific Hsp47 knockout in HFD-fed mice reduced adipocyte size and fibrosis markers specifically in inguinal WAT; metabolic deterioration was alleviated in aKO mice
comments: Depot-specific effects are notable, but mechanistic links between collagen-integrin signaling and inflammatory gene expression warrant further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hsp47 promotes pro-inflammatory gene expression in ingWAT during HFD
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Hsp47 aKO reduced inflammatory markers and fibrosis in ingWAT but not epiWAT

title: ROS-NLRP3 participates in the pyroptosis response of excretory-secretory products from protoscoleces of Echinococcus granulosus in hepatocytes
task: Determine whether E. granulosus excretory-secretory products induce hepatocyte pyroptosis via ROS-NLRP3 inflammasome
key_results: ESP treatment reduced hepatocyte viability, increased LDH release and pyroptosis markers; NLRP3 inhibition (MCC950) attenuated ESP-induced cytotoxicity; ROS scavenger (NAC) suppressed pyroptosis and inflammasome activation
comments: The ROS-NLRP3 pathway involvement is well-demonstrated, though translation to understanding hepatic cystic echinococcosis pathogenesis in vivo requires validation in infected animal models.

model_io.model: AML-12 hepatocytes exposed to E. granulosus ESPs

hypotheses.hypothesis: ESP-induced pyroptosis is mediated by ROS-NLRP3 inflammasome activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: NLRP3 inhibition and ROS scavenging both blocked ESP-induced pyroptosis

title: The redundant protein synthesis gene Aimp1 challenges the canonical inverse relationship between translation and autophagy
task: Identify non-essential, redundant protein synthesis genes that limit autophagy independently of mTORC1
key_results: Aimp1 depletion in murine myeloid cells increased autophagy while minimally affecting protein synthesis; Aimp1 limits autophagy through uncoupling of mTORC1; immune response kinetics were impaired in Aimp1-depleted cells
comments: The functional redundancy of protein synthesis genes presents a paradigm exception to the canonical inverse relationship; however, the physiological significance of autophagy enhancement during immunity needs clarification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aimp1 limits autophagy through uncoupling of mTORC1 despite redundancy in protein synthesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Aimp1 depletion increased autophagy while minimally affecting translation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Protein synthesis and autophagy are inversely related during immune responses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: transcriptomics demonstrated inverse relationship during immune activation

title: International testing and refinement of AI algorithms predicting acute leukemia subtypes from routine laboratory data
task: Develop AI algorithm for acute leukemia subtype prediction from routine laboratory data
key_results: Ensemble (Isolation Forest + LOF) achieves AUROC 0.84 for AML (vs 0.72 baseline) while excluding only 12.1% vs 70.8–92.5% with confidence cutoff; generalizes across 20 international centers; pediatric retraining improves performance
comments: Addresses real clinical disparity; ensemble approach improves robustness on low-confidence cases; external validation across diverse centers strengthens generalizability claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble methods improve accuracy and robustness on cases below pretrained confidence threshold
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AUROC improved 0.72→0.84 for AML while reducing exclusion from 70.8% to 12.1%

title: High-resolution phage-host assignment through key proteins using large language models
task: Phage-host assignment via protein language models and key protein features
key_results: VirHost Hunter doubles phage-host predictions vs baselines; identifies previously uncharacterized phages targeting Akkermansia and Prevotella; synthesized anti-obesity bacterial lysin
comments: Protein language models overcome sequence dissimilarity effectively; gut phage lysin therapeutic has proof-of-concept but clinical development untested.

model_io.model: VirHost Hunter (protein language models + vision transformers)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Protein language models capture functional homology despite sequence dissimilarity for phage-host assignment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves 2-fold improvement over existing methods

title: Ultra-precision deconvolution of spatial transcriptomics decodes immune heterogeneity and fate-defining programs in tissues
task: Spatial transcriptomics deconvolution to map immune cell subpopulations
key_results: UCASpatial identifies low-abundant subpopulations with superior accuracy; reveals chromosome 20q gain drives T cell exclusion in colorectal cancer; identifies IL11-IL11RA signaling driving pro-fibrotic community in wound healing
comments: Entropy-based weighting approach shows promise but validation is limited to two biological contexts; mechanistic insights into immune-fibroblast crosstalk are suggestive rather than definitive.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chromosome 20q gain in cancer clones excludes T cells through HERV-H silencing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; associated with impaired type I interferon responses

hypotheses.hypothesis: IL11-IL11RA signaling in pro-fibrotic community drives scarring phenotype
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in murine wound healing model

title: A disease-agnostic approach to ensemble learning for infectious disease forecasting
task: Disease-agnostic ensemble forecasting for emerging infectious diseases
key_results: epiFFORMA outperforms equal-weighting on 10 diseases including COVID-19, influenza, and dengue; weighted strategy uses synthetic epidemiological data without historical training data
comments: Strong generalization across diverse pathogens; synthetic data approach is clever but real-time forecasting performance on truly novel pathogens remains untested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Data-free ensembling based on epidemiological dynamics outperforms naive equal-weighting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across 10 infectious diseases

title: Intrinsic mechanisms and microenvironmental cues fine-tune plasticity of esophageal progenitors
task: Dissect epithelial-stromal crosstalk regulating esophageal progenitor plasticity
key_results: Hedgehog regulates Sox9 via epithelial-stromal TGF-β/BMP signaling and cell-intrinsic Cox-2 mechanism; Cox-2 inhibitors suppress plasticity in mouse and human models
comments: Elegant dissection of direct and indirect regulatory mechanisms; Cox-2 as a therapeutic target for metaplasia prevention is promising but tested only in vitro.

model_io.model: Mouse genetic + pharmacological + cell culture models

hypotheses.hypothesis: TGF-β and BMP synergistically induce Sox9 and metaplasia-prone transcriptomic state
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in mouse and human models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cox-2 is a cell-intrinsic modulator of Hedgehog-induced plasticity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Cox-2 inhibitors suppress Sox9 and plasticity

title: Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life
task: Synthesize advances toward generalist biological AI for modeling information flow across biomolecules
key_results: Review charts path toward concurrent DNA, RNA, protein and cellular modeling; identifies opportunities in synergizing language and structural models; highlights scalability and validation challenges
comments: Comprehensive vision of convergent AI methodologies; concrete near-term milestones and technical bottlenecks not articulated; speculative on timeline to integrated virtual cells.

title: Open and sustainable AI: challenges, opportunities and the road ahead in the life sciences
task: Framework for open and sustainable AI in life sciences
key_results: Reviewed 300+ ecosystem components; provides implementation pathways for reproducible, reusable AI; addresses trust, sustainability and fragmentation in AI adoption
comments: Perspective rather than empirical validation; recommendations are practical but lack quantitative evidence of downstream impact on research outcomes.

title: LazySlide: accessible and interoperable whole-slide image analysis
task: Bridge histopathology with omics through whole-slide image analysis
key_results: LazySlide integrates vision-language models with scverse standards; supports tissue/cell segmentation, zero-shot classification and cross-modal querying
comments: Open-source software contribution that standardizes histopathology workflows; minimal quantitative benchmarking presented.

title: Backpropagation-free spiking neural networks with the forward–forward algorithm
task: Train spiking neural networks via forward-forward algorithm as backpropagation-free alternative
key_results: FF-SNN achieves accuracy comparable to backpropagation SNNs on static datasets; outperforms on complex spiking task (SHD); lighter architecture with enhanced computational efficiency
comments: Biological plausibility and neuromorphic hardware compatibility advantages; empirical gains modest on standard benchmarks; scalability to larger neuromorphic applications unclear.

inputs: MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, Kuzushiji-MNIST, Neuro-MNIST, SHD spiking datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: FF algorithm enables layer-wise localized SNN learning competitive with backpropagation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; accuracy comparable on static datasets, superior on SHD spiking task

title: Meta-analysis, WGCNA, and machine learning converge on a four-gene biomarker panel for heat stress tolerance in Solanum lycopersicum
task: Meta-analysis and machine learning identify heat stress tolerance biomarkers in tomato
key_results: Four-gene signature (Solyc09g074500, Solyc01g102960, Solyc07g042230, Solyc02g091990) achieves 98.5% classification accuracy and AUC>0.95 across datasets
comments: High accuracy on transcriptomic benchmarks; signature reflects canonical heat-shock response but functional validation and breeding utility not demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integrating meta-analysis, coexpression, and machine learning identifies robust heat-tolerance biomarkers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; four-gene panel achieves 98.5% classification accuracy

title: Statistical models to characterize colon tumor stiffness heterogeneity through representative atomic force microscopy maps
task: Link tissue stiffness heterogeneity to clinical parameters in colon cancer
key_results: AFM-derived stiffness correlates with RAS mutations, tumor location, microsatellite instability; machine learning infers clinical phenotypes from stiffness data
comments: Integrates biophysical measurement with machine learning; novel associations with RAS and DNA repair defects are interesting but mechanism remains unexplored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tumor stiffness encodes genotype and clinical stage information
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; correlations with RAS, location, microsatellite instability but causality unclear

title: Exploring exosome-related genes as candidate biomarkers in primary immune thrombocytopenia through transcriptomics and preliminary experimental validation
task: Identify exosome-related gene candidates in primary immune thrombocytopenia
key_results: GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, GSR downregulated in ITP patients (P<0.05); molecular docking suggests drug-binding feasibility; RT-qPCR confirms dysregulation
comments: Exploratory study combining bioinformatics and clinical validation; HIBADH predicted but not validated experimentally, weakening confidence in gene panel.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosome-related genes dysregulated in ITP correlate with T-cell dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, HIBADH correlate with T-cell subsets but HIBADH not experimentally validated

title: Identification and verification of the key genes, CCR1 and EGR2, in diabetes-associated lipophagy
task: Identify lipophagy-associated genes in diabetes via meta-analysis and WGCNA
key_results: CCR1 and EGR2 identified as hub genes; both upregulated in diabetic samples (P<0.05); CCR1-/- mice show improved glucose homeostasis on high-fat diet
comments: Integration of multiple computational methods is rigorous; experimental validation in mice is encouraging but human therapeutic utility remains speculative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCR1 and EGR2 are biomarkers of diabetes-associated lipophagy dysregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; both elevated in serum and liver tissue; CCR1 knockout improves glucose homeostasis

title: A convolutional attention model classifies copy number variants from whole exome sequencing
task: Classify copy number variants from whole-exome sequencing using attention-based CNN
key_results: CNN-Att achieves macro F1=0.83, macro PR-AUC=0.93 on held-out WES data; cross-platform F1 up to 0.96 (HiSeq, NovaSeq, MGISEQ, BGISEQ); robust generalization across sequencing technologies
comments: Attention mechanism highlights weak depth signals for small events; sensitivity-precision tradeoff well-characterized; clinical utility requires validation against gold-standard karyotyping.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNN attention architecture generalizes across sequencing platforms better than read-depth heuristics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; F1 up to 0.96 across four platform types

title: Optimization of cross-institutional medical federated learning framework driven by confidential computing
task: Optimize federated learning for privacy-sensitive medical data under TEE vulnerabilities
key_results: CC-PGD reduces privacy leakage by 23–31% and communication cost by 18–27% vs baselines while maintaining ≤2% accuracy loss on MNIST/CIFAR-10
comments: First framework explicitly modeling TEE side-channel risks with convergence proof (O(1/√T)); experiments on standard benchmarks rather than authentic medical data limit clinical relevance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint optimization of privacy and communication outperforms independent objectives
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CC-PGD achieves better privacy-communication-accuracy tradeoffs than FedAvg, DP-FL, FedProx

title: Design of robust networks via reinforcement learning prompts the emergence of multi-backbones
task: Design robust networks via reinforcement learning
key_results: Framework surpasses existing methods for robust network design under multidimensional attacks; discovers emergent multi-backbone topologies during optimization
comments: Novel RL-based approach reveals interesting higher-order network structures, though scalability to very large networks remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL-based design can discover robust network topologies better than Monte Carlo and manual approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; framework surpasses existing methods

hypotheses.hypothesis: Network optimization induces multi-backbone emergence as a robustness strategy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multi-backbones develop during design process

title: High-Resolution Downscaled CMIP6 Projections dataset of Key Climate Variables for Senegal
task: High-resolution climate projections downscaling for Senegal
key_results: 0.0375° × 0.0375° spatial resolution dataset from 19 CMIP6 models; daily data 1850–2100 across three emissions scenarios with bias correction
comments: CDF-transform downscaling is standard practice; dataset quality and rigorous metadata/outlier handling strengthen utility, though validation against local observations would strengthen credibility.

outputs: high-resolution daily climate variables (temperature, precipitation, radiation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CDF-transform bias correction improves downscaled projection accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via methodology; dataset produced with rigorous quality control

title: Performance analysis of network automation techniques for dense IP networks
task: Performance analysis of network automation techniques for dense IP networks
key_results: Automated topology creation 4.5× faster than manual; configuration generation ~10% faster; test execution 11× faster than manual testing
comments: Practical benchmarking of NA tools (EVE-NG, Pllama, ContainerLab) across topology, configuration, testing stages; limited discussion of failure recovery and complex network scenarios.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Automated topology creation is significantly faster than manual approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 4.5× speedup achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Test automation via NETCONF/CLI outperforms manual testing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 11× faster execution

title: Proactive fault prediction in marine diesel engines using multivariate machine learning
task: Proactive fault prediction in marine diesel engines
key_results: ConvLSTM model reduces RMSE by 15.45% vs. decision trees; RF classifier achieves 82.168% accuracy for fault classification
comments: Multivariate time series ML for maritime maintenance is practical and valuable; however, generalization to different engine types and failure modes requires larger datasets.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ConvLSTM captures spatiotemporal patterns in multivariate engine sensor data better than decision trees
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 15.45% RMSE improvement

hypotheses.hypothesis: RF classification reliably predicts engine faults from sensor features
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 82.168% accuracy achieved

title: Sampled-data control under time-varying delays: a robust approach for high-renewable smart grids
task: Robust sampled-data control for high-renewable smart grids with time-varying delays
key_results: Adaptive controller reduces settling time by 33%, overshoot by 52%, energy cost by 40% vs. fixed-gain baselines; maintains stability under combined delay, jitter, packet loss
comments: Physics-informed adaptive control linking communication quality to feedback gain is elegant; practical deployment on real microgrids and sensitivity to parameter estimation errors need validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Online-measurable delay-jitter index enables effective real-time controller adaptation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 33% settling time and 52% overshoot reduction achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lyapunov–Krasovskii framework certifies stability under combined communication impairments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; exponential stability proven under delay, jitter, packet loss

title: Comparative evaluation of YOLO and RT-DETR models for real-time defect detection in wood-based 3D printing
task: Real-time defect detection in wood-based 3D printing
key_results: YOLOv11 highest precision (0.632); RT-DETR highest mAP@0.5 (0.563); YOLOv8 best speed-accuracy tradeoff (3.2 ms inference) on 599-image dataset
comments: Systematic comparison of five YOLO variants and RT-DETR highlights practical inference speed-accuracy tradeoffs; dataset size (599 images) is modest for deep learning validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv11 achieves highest precision for defect detection in wood binder jetting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 0.632 precision on test set

hypotheses.hypothesis: RT-DETR achieves higher mAP but slower inference than YOLO variants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mAP@0.5=0.563 vs. 3.2–17.9 ms inference times

title: Research on the atomic clock signal denoising method based on the hyperbolic tangent smooth threshold function
task: Atomic clock signal denoising via hyperbolic tangent smooth thresholding
key_results: Proposed method increases SNR by 14–26% and reduces RMSE by 10–28% vs. soft thresholding on cesium/hydrogen/rubidium clocks
comments: Novel smooth threshold function elegantly bridges hard/soft thresholding discontinuities; good empirical validation but theoretical convergence guarantees absent.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hyperbolic tangent function provides continuous transition between hard/soft thresholding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved SNR and RMSE over traditional methods

hypotheses.hypothesis: SURE criterion optimizes threshold per IMF
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enhanced frequency stability of time scale achieved

title: Adaptive feature selection with gradient-based relevance for intrusion detection systems
task: Adaptive feature selection for intrusion detection in energy systems
key_results: AFS improves detection accuracy by 24.74% and reduces training time by 35% vs. PCA-based methods on CIRA-CIC-DoHBrw-2020 dataset
comments: Gradient-based relevance is a smart enhancement over statistical methods for capturing context-sensitive traffic; however, computational overhead of gradient computation and scalability to larger feature spaces unclear.

model_io.model: Adaptive Feature Selection (AFS) with Gradient-Based Relevance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gradient-based relevance captures context-sensitive malicious patterns better than statistical methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 24.74% accuracy improvement over PCA

hypotheses.hypothesis: AFS reduces training overhead via effective dimensionality reduction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 35% training time reduction

title: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment via physics-guided piecewise-linear nadir surrogates and adaptive virtual inertia
task: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment with physics-guided surrogates
key_results: FC-RUC reduces operating cost by 24.4% (IEEE 9-bus) and 27.5% (IEEE 118-bus) while satisfying RoCoF, nadir, and QSS frequency constraints
comments: Physics-guided piecewise-linear nadir surrogate elegantly encodes frequency dynamics into UC without full N–k enumeration; practical applicability to larger grids and real-time implementation need assessment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-guided surrogates conservatively approximate nadir dynamics without enumeration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; frequency constraints satisfied and cost reduced

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive virtual inertia provision enables feasible synthetic inertia from renewables
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; headroom-coupled constraints ensure deliverability

title: SVDHLA: symmetric variable depth hybrid learning automaton and its application
task: Symmetric variable-depth hybrid learning automaton with adaptive exploration-exploitation
key_results: SVDHLA self-adaptively adjusts depth parameter; outperforms state-of-the-art LA in stationary/non-stationary environments; LA-based dropout improves neural network regularization
comments: Novel hybrid combining L_KN,K with variable action set automaton addresses depth parameter tuning; limited experimental domains and unclear sensitivity to environment stationarity assumptions.

model_io.model: SVDHLA (Symmetric Variable Depth Hybrid Learning Automaton)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-adaptive depth adjustment balances exploration-exploitation better than fixed depth
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior cumulative reward and regret in simulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: LA-based dropout prevents overfitting better than previous LA methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved neural network performance demonstrated

title: Information theory and thermal properties of an extended cosine hyperbolic potential model
task: Information theory and thermal properties of extended cosine hyperbolic potential
key_results: Fisher information and Shannon entropy derived analytically; Cramér-Rao bound and BBM inequality confirmed; molar thermodynamic properties (H, S, G, C_p) match NIST data with ≤26% discrepancy
comments: Rigorous information-theoretic treatment with good thermochemical validation on diatomic molecules; however, generalization to polyatomic systems and potential functional form limitations unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extended cosine hyperbolic potential satisfies information-theoretic inequalities
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Cramér-Rao bound and BBM inequality verified

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model predicts molar thermodynamic properties consistent with NIST data
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; excellent agreement across 0–6000 K temperature range

title: A hybrid unified decoding analytics system for end-of-life strategies in mass-produced EV battery projects
task: Multi-criteria decision-making for EV battery end-of-life strategies
key_results: Cipher fuzzy set model identifies circular value-add and energy efficiency as top criteria; component reuse and cathode-to-cathode recycling as optimal strategies; eliminates defuzzification information loss
comments: Novel cipher fuzzy set framework for EV battery circularity decisions is methodologically sound; however, empirical data availability for sensitivity analysis and real-world stakeholder validation limited.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cipher fuzzy sets reduce information loss vs. traditional defuzzification methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; framework eliminates defuzzification step

hypotheses.hypothesis: Circular value-add and energy efficiency emerge as critical investment criteria
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; identified as top priorities via aggregation

title: Adaptive graph signal processing for robust multimodal fusion with dynamic semantic alignment
task: Multimodal fusion via adaptive graph signal processing with dynamic semantic alignment
key_results: AGSP-DSA achieves 95.3% accuracy on CMU-MOSEI, 93.4% on AudioVisual Event dataset, 91.8% on MMDb; outperforms MultiModal GNN by 2.6% accuracy
comments: Dual-graph construction and spectral filtering for multimodal fusion is principled; dynamic semantic alignment is novel, though computational complexity and missing modality robustness need further analysis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-graph learning captures intra-model and inter-modal relations effectively
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; state-of-the-art results on three benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic semantic alignment improves robustness to missing modalities
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; good generalization in missing modality settings

title: Dynamic intrusion detection for internet of drones using glowworm swarm optimization
task: Dynamic intrusion detection for Internet of Drones using bio-inspired optimization
key_results: DIGS-RTD framework reduces false positives and processing time vs. STFA baseline; superior computational cost-detection accuracy tradeoff
comments: Glowworm Swarm Optimization for feature selection is creative, though the paper lacks detailed comparison metrics and scalability testing on large drone networks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GSO multimodal optimization locates diverse intrusion patterns effectively
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior detection performance vs. baseline methods

hypotheses.hypothesis: Active feature selection reduces computational load without accuracy loss
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced processing time with maintained detection accuracy

title: Integrated approach for edge coverage enhancement based on IRS phase shift control and AP selection in dense user communication system
task: Edge coverage enhancement via IRS and cell-free MIMO
key_results: SINR gain of 4 dB for cell-center users, 2.4 dB for cell-edge users with AP selection algorithm
comments: Integrated framework combining IRSs, massive MIMO, and NOMA addresses dense user coverage, though practical IRS deployment constraints need further validation.

model_io.model: IRS Phase Shift Optimization (AO and SDR methods)

hypotheses.hypothesis: SDR and AO achieve similar convergence but differ in complexity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; comparative analysis shows tradeoff between methods

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic AP selection reduces overhead without sacrificing SINR
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2.4–4 dB SINR gains achieved with reduced resources

title: AI-assisted reliability-based design framework for tunnel concrete linings in weak rocks
task: Reliability-based design of tunnel concrete linings using AI surrogates and CCM
key_results: AI surrogate reproduces probabilistic trends with high consistency; FRPC reduces failure probability substantially at intermediate thicknesses; RBD framework achieves target β/P_f levels
comments: Integration of CCM, FORM, and data-driven surrogates is rigorous; however, epistemic uncertainty in geomechanical parameters and model validation on diverse weak rock types remain limitations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI surrogates accurately replicate CCM–reliability probabilistic trends
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; surrogate shows high consistency with slight conservatism

hypotheses.hypothesis: FRPC significantly improves reliability at intermediate thicknesses vs. conventional concrete

title: Lightweight model LMW-YOLO for small object detection in remote sensing images
task: Small object detection in remote sensing images

comments: Lightweight design via CSD strategy and novel modules (LKCA, MSDP, WIoU v3) effectively addresses small target detection trade-offs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decomposed LKA in shallow layers captures long-range dependencies for small targets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LKCA module improves detection on small objects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic WIoU v3 loss accelerates convergence in dense scenarios
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enhanced localization accuracy observed

title: Multi-center chameleon hashing based Blockchain integrated digital copyright transaction scheme for data redacting in Blockchain based IoT systems
task: Blockchain-based digital copyright transaction with multi-center chameleon hashing
key_results: Scheme supports data redaction via chameleon hashing; flexible permission node management with optimized attribute encryption ensures confidentiality and malicious node resistance
comments: Chameleon hashing for copyright data modification is clever, though scalability and gas cost implications for blockchain deployment are not discussed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-center chameleon hashing enables efficient data redaction in copyright transfers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; scheme supports modification while maintaining security

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flexible node permission mechanism resists malicious actors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; attribute encryption ensures confidentiality and accountability

title: Observation of partonic flow in proton—proton and proton—nucleus collisions
task: Demonstrate quark-gluon plasma formation in proton-proton and proton-nucleus collisions via partonic flow
key_results: Distinctive baryon-meson anisotropic flow grouping observed in high-multiplicity pp and pPb collisions at LHC; consistent with hydrodynamic flow and quark coalescence mechanism
comments: Extension of QGP signatures to small systems strengthens evidence for partonic matter formation beyond heavy-ion collisions.

model_io.model: High-energy pp and pPb collisions with hydrodynamic + quark coalescence hadronization

hypotheses.hypothesis: QGP-like partonic flow occurs in small pp and pPb systems with distinctive baryon-meson ordering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; flow grouping matches predictions of hydrodynamic coalescence model

title: Giant magneto-cubic in-plane Hall effect in a nonmagnetic material
task: Discover giant magneto-cubic in-plane Hall effect in nonmagnetic half-Heusler compound
key_results: Giant in-plane Hall effect in LuAuSn exceeding all prior reports; 2π/3-periodic cubic field dependence; dominated by extrinsic side jump and skew scattering
comments: Unexpected discovery of giant nonlinear Hall effect in nonmagnetic material opens new materials platform for applications.

model_io.model: First-principles calculations + side jump and skew scattering analysis for LuAuSn
inputs: In-plane magnetic field under threefold rotational symmetry

hypotheses.hypothesis: Giant magneto-cubic in-plane Hall effect arises in nonmagnetic material with threefold symmetry via extrinsic scattering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; magnitude exceeds prior reports; scaling and symmetry verified

title: Confined-hydrogel fluidic memristor crossbar array for neuromorphic computing
task: Develop scalable fluidic memristor array for neuromorphic computing using confined hydrogel
key_results: 10×10 fluidic memristor array exhibits paired-pulse facilitation/depression, spike-rate-dependent plasticity; 89.5% classification accuracy on MNIST dataset
comments: Clever confined-hydrogel design overcomes scalability challenges, though neuromorphic performance remains moderate compared to electronic systems.

model_io.model: Confined hydrogel fluidic memristor array on polyimide micropores with ionic transport

hypotheses.hypothesis: Confined hydrogel interface enables scalable 10×10 array with neuromorphic plasticity behaviors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; array demonstrates facilitation/depression and achieves 89.5% MNIST accuracy

title: Physics-informed Hamiltonian learning for large-scale optoelectronic property prediction
task: Predict optoelectronic properties of large-scale halide perovskites using physics-informed Hamiltonian learning
key_results: HAMSTER framework captures temperature and compositional effects with few first-principles calculations; scales to tens-of-thousands-atom systems; achieves data efficiency and physical interpretability
comments: Elegant hybrid physics-learning approach balancing interpretability and accuracy for practical materials screening.

model_io.model: Physics-informed machine learning (HAMSTER) starting from approximate Hamiltonian model and refining with few DFT calculations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-informed Hamiltonian learning provides accurate optoelectronic predictions with high data efficiency and transferability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HAMSTER scales to 10k-atom systems with accuracy and interpretability

title: Grain boundary effects on radiation damage and tritium diffusion in Li–Al–O ceramics from molecular dynamics and experiments
task: Understand grain boundary effects on radiation damage and tritium transport in LiAlO2 ceramics
key_results: Grain boundaries suppress damage via interstitial emission (especially Li defects) but promote tritium diffusion by 2–10× due to higher free volume; trade-off identified for TPBAR design
comments: Molecular dynamics reveals competing microstructural effects that must be balanced in tritium breeding blanket optimization.

model_io.model: Molecular dynamics simulations of LiAlO2 and LiAl5O8 single crystals and bicrystals at 600 K

hypotheses.hypothesis: Grain boundaries reduce radiation damage accumulation in LiAlO2 but accelerate tritium migration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; GBs suppress Li defects but increase tritium diffusivity 2–10-fold

title: Fermi liquid and isotropic superconductivity of Hund scenario for bilayer nickelates
task: Compare Hund-driven versus hybridization-driven superconductivity mechanisms in bilayer nickelates
key_results: Hund scenario yields isotropic s±-wave gap, lower Tc, Fermi liquid normal state; differs fundamentally from hybridization mechanism; Hund alone insufficient for bulk and thin-film data
comments: Systematic theoretical comparison using same method clarifies competing pairing mechanisms but suggests multi-mechanism origin.

model_io.model: Dynamic Schwinger boson mean-field theory for bilayer nickelate Hund and hybridization scenarios

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hund coupling alone drives isotropic s±-wave superconductivity with lower Tc and Fermi liquid normal state
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; mechanism differs from hybridization but experimental Tc values suggest mixed mechanism

title: Orders-of-magnitude improvement in precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
task: Achieve high-precision spectroscopy of inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
key_results: ~100-fold improvement in precision over prior work; coherent Rabi oscillations observed; isotope shift measurements below 10 Hz uncertainty; constraints on boson-mediated electron-neutron force
comments: Demonstration of magic-wavelength lattice trapping enabling transformative precision gains for both fundamental physics and frequency metrology.

model_io.model: Neutral Yb atoms in 3D magic-wavelength optical lattice with spectroscopy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magic-wavelength lattice enables two-orders-of-magnitude precision improvement for inner-shell clock transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Rabi coherence and sub-10-Hz isotope shift measurements achieved

title: Exceptional sensitivity near the bistable transition point of a hybrid quantum system
task: Enhance magnetometry sensitivity using nonlinear bistable dynamics near exceptional points
key_results: 17-fold signal-to-noise ratio enhancement in nitrogen-vacancy magnetometry near bistable transition; sensitivity exceeds ideal bare electron magnetometer
comments: Clever exploitation of nonlinearity to overcome fundamental noise amplification limitation of linear exceptional points.

model_io.model: Hybrid quantum system: NV center coupled to van der Pol oscillator

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinear bistability can enhance sensing signal-to-noise ratio compared to linear exceptional points
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 17-fold SNR enhancement and sensitivity exceeding ideal magnetometer

title: First positronium lifetime imaging using 52Mn and 55Co with a plastic-scintillator-based PET scanner
task: Enable positronium lifetime imaging using ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co with plastic-scintillator PET scanner
key_results: First PLI measurements with ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co; ortho-positronium lifetime matches certified reference values in polycarbonate; ⁵⁵Co provides cleaner data despite shorter half-life
comments: Practical demonstration expanding positronium imaging isotope options; ⁵⁵Co favored for spectral purity despite ⁵²Mn's longer half-life.

model_io.model: Modular J-PET scanner with ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co sources for triple-coincidence PLI detection

hypotheses.hypothesis: ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co enable accurate positronium lifetime imaging with ⁵⁵Co providing superior spectral purity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ⁵⁵Co matches certified values with cleaner spectra despite ⁵²Mn's longer half-life advantage

title: Mirrorless open cavities enabled by boundary incompatibility between perfect electric conductor and perfect magnetic conductor parallel-plate waveguides
task: Achieve electromagnetic field confinement without physical mirrors using PEC-PMC waveguide boundary incompatibility
key_results: Open-geometry mirrorless cavity with mode-gap confinement; quality factor Q ~ 10² with realistic losses; enhanced magnetic-dipole Purcell factor
comments: Elegant conceptual advance enabling atom-cavity coupling in open geometries without boundary perturbation.

model_io.model: PEC-PMC parallel-plate waveguide cavity with metallic-groove artificial magnetic conductor

hypotheses.hypothesis: Boundary incompatibility between PEC and PMC creates mode gap enabling mirrorless confinement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; open cavity achieves Q ~ 10² and strong field localization

title: A novel double defected ground structures and parasitic patches for enhanced MIMO antenna performance
task: Enhance MIMO antenna performance using double defected ground structures and parasitic patches
key_results: Impedance bandwidth 1.05 GHz (S11 < −10 dB) at 5.5–6.5 GHz; mutual coupling −32 to −52 dB; envelope correlation coefficient < 0.0035; diversity gain > 9.992 dB
comments: Standard antenna engineering achieving good diversity metrics through systematic defect and parasitic element placement.

model_io.model: Four-element MIMO antenna array on FR4 substrate with DGS and parasitic patches

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGS 1 and 2 with parasitic patches improve reflection/transmission coefficients and diversity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; measured S-parameters and diversity metrics meet C-band specifications

title: Study on the deoxygenation characteristics of RP-5 aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing inerting
task: Optimize deoxygenation of RP-5 aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing using CFD and experiments
key_results: Reducing bubble diameter 2.5→1.0 mm increases oxygen volumetric mass transfer coefficient 3.9×; elevated temperature worsens deoxygenation despite enhanced diffusion due to increased gas solubility
comments: Coupled heat-mass transfer analysis reveals counterintuitive temperature effect; practical guidance for airborne inerting system design.

model_io.model: Eulerian-Eulerian two-fluid CFD + experimental validation for gas-liquid oxygen transfer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bubble diameter and temperature competitively affect oxygen mass transfer in RP-5 scrubbing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; smaller bubbles enhance transfer 3.9-fold; elevated T worsens performance due to solubility increase

title: A single diode with integrated photosensing, memory and processing for neuromorphic image sensors
task: Create single p-n diode with integrated photosensing, memory, and processing for neuromorphic imaging
key_results: Band-structure engineered p-GaN/n-AlGaN/n-GaN nanowire diode exhibits photosensing (10.45 mA/W responsivity), photosynaptic behavior (122% paired-pulse facilitation), photo-memory (8 linear states); array enables denoising and image classification
comments: Innovative monolithic integration of three functions in single diode via electron-reservoir engineering; promising for compact neuromorphic sensors.

model_io.model: Vertically grown p-GaN/n-AlGaN/n-GaN nanowire diode with embedded electron reservoir and array integration

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bandgap-engineered electron reservoir enables single diode to integrate photosensing, memory, and processing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; diode shows responsivity, facilitation ratio, multi-state memory, and functional image processing

title: Origin of pressure-induced anomalies in the nodal-line ferrimagnet Mn3Si2Te6
task: Explain pressure-induced insulator-to-metal transition and magnetic anomalies in nodal-line ferrimagnet
key_results: DFT and Monte Carlo simulations reproduce experimental ordering temperatures and magnetic patterns; anomalous Hall conductivity requires extrinsic contributions or electron doping to explain
comments: The theoretical model successfully captures magnetic evolution but incompletely explains Hall response, suggesting missing physics or sample complexity.

model_io.model: DFT calculations + Heisenberg Hamiltonians extraction + Classical Monte Carlo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pressure-induced IMT drives magnetic anomalies via changes in electronic structure
hypotheses.result: Supported for ordering temperature but not for anomalous Hall conductivity magnitude
